title: Container ID 42 cannot be mapped to a host ID
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
In a debug session with Valentin working around "--userns=keep-id" issues, the workaround "-user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1" was proposed, but sadly does not show the expected results.

Configuration changes, such as

- default_sysctls = [] in containers.conf
- ignore_chown_errors in storage.conf 

seem to have no impact, details on the used configuration are shared in "Additional information".

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ podman run --user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1 --rm alpine id
Error: error creating container storage: error creating an ID-mapped copy of layer "e5e13b0c77cbb769548077189c3da2f0a764ceca06af49d8d558e759f5c232bd": exit status 1: error during chown: error mapping container ID pair idtools.IDPair{UID:0, GID:42} for "etc/shadow" to host: Container ID 42 cannot be mapped to a host ID
 
# a pull beforehand does not change the behavior:

[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ podman pull alpine
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob c158987b0551 done
Copying config 49176f190c done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
49176f190c7e9cdb51ac85ab6c6d5e4512352218190cd69b08e6fd803ffbf3da
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ podman run --user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1 --rm alpine id
Error: error creating container storage: error creating an ID-mapped copy of layer "ded7a220bb058e28ee3254fbba04ca90b679070424424761a53a043b93b612bf": exit status 1: error during chown: error mapping container ID pair idtools.IDPair{UID:0, GID:42} for "etc/shadow" to host: Container ID 42 cannot be mapped to a host ID
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Container does not start, exits with the following exit status:
```
exit status 1: error during chown: error mapping container ID pair idtools.IDPair{UID:0, GID:42} for "etc/shadow" to host: Container ID 42 cannot be mapped to a host ID
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Container starts

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
```
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ cat /etc/redhat-release
Rocky Linux release 8.5 (Green Obsidian)
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ grep -v \# /etc/subuid
holgrrr:100000:1024
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ grep -v \# /etc/subgid
holgrrr:100000:1024
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ cat /etc/containers/containers.conf
[containers]
  default_sysctls = []
 
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ grep ^ignore /etc/containers/storage.conf
ignore_chown_errors = "true"
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.12
Built:        Wed Feb  2 00:59:28 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


No. After upgrading to the latest version the error message changes, will share that in an additional comment.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
KVM-based test VM

comments.body:
After upgrading to the latest version of podman in the repo (yum update podman) the error message changes to 
`Error: OCI runtime error: runc: User namespaces enabled, but no user mapping found.`

```
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ cat /etc/redhat-release
Rocky Linux release 8.5 (Green Obsidian)
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.2.0-4.module+el8.7.0+1078+e72fcd4f.x86_64
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ grep -v \# /etc/subuid
holgrrr:100000:1024
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ grep -v \# /etc/subgid
holgrrr:100000:1024
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ cat /etc/containers/containers.conf
[containers]
  default_sysctls = []

[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ grep ^ignore /etc/containers/storage.conf
ignore_chown_errors = "true"
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ podman -v
podman version 4.2.0
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ podman run --user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1 --rm alpine id
Error: OCI runtime error: runc: User namespaces enabled, but no user mapping found.


[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ # again with the pull beforehand
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ podman pull alpine
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob c158987b0551 skipped: already exists
Copying config 49176f190c done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
49176f190c7e9cdb51ac85ab6c6d5e4512352218190cd69b08e6fd803ffbf3da
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ podman run --user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1 --rm alpine id
Error: OCI runtime error: runc: User namespaces enabled, but no user mapping found.
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$
```
@giuseppe that's the issue I pinged you on recently.  I asked to open an issue here to open up the conversation.

not sure what the expectation is with `podman run --user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1 --rm alpine id`, should all files be squashed to the same UID or left unmapped?  Probably the latter since a configuration like `--uidmap=$UID:0:2` would then be confusing as we can chown to two different IDs.

We have `ignore_chown_errors = "true"` but that means there the IDs space is limited to what is allocated to your user, and anything that doesn't fit in it is owned by root.


In the case of having only a single ID available, everything is owned by root, so the command to use would be:  `podman run --user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1 --rm alpine id`

Since you have 1025 UIDs available images are stored using these IDs.  Why not to use them?

Background story: this originally comes from an enterprise HPC environment, that would love to be able to have the subXid files unpopulated for the maintenance effort and were also not happy with maintaining the ranges in LDAP.

The approach was then to test with --userns=keep-id, and a minimal mapping of 1 which resulted in issues as well (see below) - and Valentin proposed to make use of `-user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1` as workaround, as it would provide a similar behavior (at least my understanding).

```
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ cat /etc/redhat-release ​
Rocky Linux release 8.5 (Green Obsidian)​

[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ podman -v​
podman version 3.4.2​

[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ grep -v \# /etc/subuid​
holgrrr:100000:1​
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ grep -v \# /etc/subgid​
holgrrr:100000:1​

[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ podman run --userns=keep-id -ti alpine id​
Error: error creating container storage: error creating an ID-mapped copy of layer "e5e13b0c77cbb769548077189c3da2f0a764ceca06af49d8d558e759f5c232bd": exit status 1: error during chown: error mapping container ID pair idtools.IDPair{UID:0, GID:42} for "etc/shadow" to host: Container ID 42 cannot be mapped to a host ID​
```

I think that it will work better if you already try on an environment where there are no available sub-ids.  Could you try removing your user from `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid`?  In such case at pull time all files are owned by root, so the issue you've encountered should not happen

Sure :)


```
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ grep -v \# /etc/subgid
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ grep -v \# /etc/subuid
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ podman run --user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1 --rm alpine id
Error: OCI runtime error: runc: User namespaces enabled, but no user mapping found.
```
thanks for checking it out.  Does it work any better with crun (`podman --runtime crun run --user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1 --rm alpine id`) ?

Oh, wait a minute! :)

```
# RUNC
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ runc -v
runc version 1.0.3
spec: 1.0.2-dev
go: go1.16.12
libseccomp: 2.5.1
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ podman --runtime /usr/bin/runc run --user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1 --rm alpine id
Error: OCI runtime error: /usr/bin/runc: User namespaces enabled, but no user mapping found.

# CRUN
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ crun --version
crun version 1.5
commit: 54ebb8ca8bf7e6ddae2eb919f5b82d1d96863dea
spec: 1.0.0
+SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
[holgrrr@rocky8-container0 ~]$ podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun run --user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1 --rm alpine id
uid=1000(1000) gid=1000(1000) groups=1000(1000)
```
can we close this issue now?

Yes, I think this issue can be closed, I just wanted to share another data point from another system, that has a slightly different version setup (Podman 4.02 instead of Podman 4.2 + crun 1.4.4 instead of crun 1.5). So the desired behavior is probably due to crun 1.5.

```
$ cat /etc/redhat-release
Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 8.6 (Ootpa)

$ grep ^$USER /etc/subuid
$ grep ^$USER /etc/subgid

$ cat ~/.config/containers/containers.conf
[containers]
runtime="/usr/bin/crun"
default_sysctls = []

$ grep ignore ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
    ignore_chown_errors = "true"

$ podman -v
podman version 4.0.2

$ runc --version
runc version 1.0.3
spec: 1.0.2-dev
go: go1.17.7
libseccomp: 2.5.2

$ podman --runtime /usr/bin/runc run -it --rm --user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1 alpine:latest id
Error: error creating container storage: error creating an ID-mapped copy of layer "ded7a220bb058e28ee3254fbba04ca90b679070424424761a53a043b93b612bf": exit status 1: error during chown: error mapping container ID pair idtools.IDPair{UID:0, GID:0} for "bin" to host: Container ID 0 cannot be mapped to a host ID

$ crun --version
crun version 1.4.4
commit: 6521fcc5806f20f6187eb933f9f45130c86da230
spec: 1.0.0
+SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL

$ podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun run -it --rm --user $UID:$UID --uidmap=$UID:0:1 alpine:latest id
Error: error creating container storage: error creating an ID-mapped copy of layer "ded7a220bb058e28ee3254fbba04ca90b679070424424761a53a043b93b612bf": exit status 1: error during chown: error mapping container ID pair idtools.IDPair{UID:0, GID:0} for "bin/ash" to host: Container ID 0 cannot be mapped to a host ID
```
There was a fix in Podman as well, that was probably not present in the older version you have used
